aboutPreview.title: Regina Guseinova: attorney, mediator, lecturer
aboutPreview.lead: Member of the Bar Council of the Kyiv Region. I actively practise in electronic asset declarations and anti-corruption law — supporting full declaration audits and corruption-related cases.
aboutPreview.secondary: Lecturer at the National School of Judges and the Higher School of Advocacy, a qualified mediator, and a legal commentator on television and radio. I practise in Ukraine and Poland.
credentials: Member of the Bar Council of the Kyiv Region
Lecturer at the National School of Judges and the Higher School of Advocacy
Qualified mediator and author of academic publications
Practice in Ukraine and Poland — Kraków, Warsaw

quote.text: The law works when someone who knows how to use it is by your side. That is my job.
quote.author: Regina Guseinova

nazk-checklist.excerpt: An NACP audit is a serious test even for experienced public officials. We break down what they check and how to prepare in advance.

about.title: About the attorney
about.intro: I, attorney Regina Huseinova, am a member of the Bar Council of the Kyiv Region. I actively practise in the field of electronic asset declarations.

bio.highlight: I was the first in Ukraine to create an original programme of seminars for declaration filers and attorneys practising in this field.
bio.experience: I have extensive hands-on experience supporting full audits of the electronic declarations of category A civil servants, members of parliament, holders of political office, and heads of state and municipal enterprises and institutions.
bio.practice: I advise on electronic asset declarations and represent clients in cases involving corruption offences and corruption-related crimes.
bio.teaching: Lecturer at the National School of Judges of Ukraine — I have delivered over 20 lectures to more than 400 judges from across the country. Lecturer at the Higher School of Advocacy.
bio.international: Registered with the regional Bar Council of Kraków as a “prawnik zagraniczny” (foreign lawyer). In Poland I cooperate with Kancelaria Adwokacka Leszczyńska-Wiącek Magdalena in Warsaw and the law office of Paweł Weber in Kraków.
bio.media: A legal commentator on television and radio: Inter, 1+1, Novyi Kanal, Channel 5, TRK Kyiv, Ukrainian Radio, and others.
credentials: Course “Solving problems in teams with the Adizes methodology”, 2012
Author of courses on media law and legal interaction between public authorities and the media
Academic publications: anti-corruption legislation, electronic declarations, freedom of speech, personal data protection, access to information

awards.title: Awards
awards.teaser: Distinctions from the Kyiv Region Bar Council, the UNBA, and church honours
items: Distinction of the Kyiv Region Bar Council “For devotion to the legal profession”
Honorary Distinction of the Ukrainian National Bar Association
Medal of St. Nicholas the Wonderworker
Order of St. Great Martyr Barbara

practices.title: Practices
practices.subtitle: Areas in which I provide legal assistance
practices.lead: Eight specialised areas — from anti-corruption defence to corporate disputes. Each one means deep expertise and hands-on experience.

cta.title: Not sure which area is yours?
cta.text: Book a consultation — we'll help you find the practice that fits your situation.

cta.title: Need advice on this matter?
cta.text: Describe your situation — I'll assess the prospects and propose a concrete plan of action.

electronic-declarations.title: Electronic declarations & full audit support
electronic-declarations.short: Support throughout filing electronic declarations, compliance review and defence when violations are found.

anti-corruption.title: Anti-corruption law
anti-corruption.short: Advice on anti-corruption law, representation before NABU and SAPO, and defence at the pre-trial investigation stage.

administrative-law.title: Administrative law
administrative-law.short: Challenging unlawful actions of authorities and protecting your rights in dealings with state and local government bodies.

mediation.title: Mediation
mediation.short: Out-of-court dispute resolution: negotiation, finding common ground and concluding mediation agreements without litigation.

medical-law.title: Medical law
medical-law.short: Protecting the rights of patients and medical staff, disputes with healthcare institutions and medical malpractice matters.

civil-servants-support.title: Legal support for civil servants
civil-servants-support.short: Legal protection for civil servants: disciplinary proceedings, appealing decisions and reinstatement to office.

civil-law.title: Civil law
civil-law.short: Representation in civil cases: contractual disputes, recovery of damages and inheritance matters.

criminal-law.title: Criminal law
criminal-law.short: Comprehensive defence in criminal proceedings — from the first interrogation to appealing the verdict.
